expected_benefits: Removes a small one-off helper that exists only to avoid an import cycle.
Makes result-type fallback logic easier to navigate by reducing indirection.
Keeps fallback behavior in a single canonical implementation location.
ideas.problem: `src/MLF/Elab/Run/ResultType/Fallback.hs` still contains `computeResultTypeFromAnnLocal`, explicitly noted as a local simplified helper used to avoid circular imports. That suggests a bounded cleanup opportunity: either inline it at its sole use site or replace it with a more direct dependency shape that avoids carrying a special-case local duplicate.
required_validation: Confirm the helper has a single call site or otherwise tightly bounded usage.
Run the relevant result-type/elaboration tests to verify fallback behavior is unchanged.
Check that any import adjustment does not reintroduce the original circular import.
risks: Attempting to share too much logic could recreate the circular dependency the helper was introduced to avoid.
If the local helper intentionally omits edge cases from a broader implementation, replacing it could subtly change fallback behavior.
ideas.title: Inline or narrowly eliminate `computeResultTypeFromAnnLocal`
ideas.why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: This targets only a local helper structure in fallback code. It does not touch the raw-vs-finalized canonical-constraint split, which the evidence says is thesis-sensitive and must remain intact.
ideas.why_this_is_simpler: It is a small, file-local simplification aimed at removing one compatibility-style helper rather than redesigning the result-type pipeline.

expected_benefits: Reduces public surface area if the module is only retained for tests.
Clarifies whether `src-public/MyLib.hs` is genuinely part of the supported API or just legacy scaffolding.
Potentially simplifies cabal exposure lists and maintenance burden.
ideas.problem: `src-public/MyLib.hs` is still exposed from `mlf2.cabal` and imported by `test/ConstraintGenSpec.hs`. A bounded candidate is to stop exposing it publicly if its remaining role is only to support test imports, while keeping tests working through an internal or test-only path.
required_validation: Confirm whether any non-test code depends on `MyLib` being exposed.
Update the test import path if exposure is reduced.
Run `test/ConstraintGenSpec.hs` and any packaging/build checks affected by the cabal exposure list.
risks: Changing an exposed module can be a user-visible API break.
The test may intentionally exercise the public library surface, in which case hiding the module would weaken coverage intent.
ideas.title: Reassess whether `src-public/MyLib.hs` still needs to be publicly exposed
ideas.why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: This is packaging/API-surface cleanup only. It does not alter canonical-constraint semantics or the raw-vs-finalized split that must be preserved.
ideas.why_this_is_simpler: It is a tightly bounded exposure decision around one module and one known test import, not a deeper architectural refactor.

expected_benefits: Makes compatibility shims more explicit and easier to retire later.
Reduces surprise around why legacy exports still exist.
Could shrink the compatibility surface if downstream use is already gone.
ideas.problem: `AGENTS.md` says `MLF.Elab.Legacy` re-exports `expansionToInst` for compatibility. A plausible simplification candidate is to verify whether that re-export is still needed, and if not, remove just that compatibility export or isolate it more clearly.
required_validation: Check whether any internal modules, tests, or documented entry points still rely on `MLF.Elab.Legacy.expansionToInst`.
If retained, verify that its compatibility purpose is documented precisely; if removed, run affected build/tests.
Ensure no downstream-facing compatibility promise is being broken unintentionally.
risks: Removing a compatibility re-export may break existing consumers.
Because the evidence comes from project guidance, the export may be intentionally preserved even if local uses are absent.
ideas.title: Audit the narrow compatibility re-export of `expansionToInst`
ideas.why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: This candidate concerns module exports and compatibility layering, not solver/elaboration semantics. The thesis-sensitive canonical split remains untouched.
ideas.why_this_is_simpler: It focuses on one named re-export rather than broad legacy cleanup, so the change can be evaluated and possibly rejected in isolation.

expected_benefits: Prevents accidental drift into disallowed recursion-schemes refactors.
Narrows future simplification discussion to verifier-approved areas.
Makes the project’s simplification boundary easier to follow during later rounds.
ideas.problem: `implementation_notes.md` row-7/row-8 already says graph-sensitive modules are not broad recursion-schemes targets, and only `safe fold` or `already recursion-schemes-backed` entries are in scope absent verifier reclassification. A bounded simplification candidate is to tighten the wording so skip-check rounds do not keep revisiting out-of-scope recursion refactors.
required_validation: Confirm the revised wording matches the existing guardrail intent exactly.
Verify no currently discussed module is being silently reclassified.
Ensure the note still leaves room for future verifier-owned audits.
risks: This may be purely process/documentation cleanup with no code simplification payoff.
Over-tight wording could block legitimate future work if it becomes too absolute.
ideas.title: Sharpen recursion-refactor guardrail wording in `implementation_notes.md`
ideas.why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: The candidate explicitly reinforces existing boundaries around where structural refactors may occur. It does not authorize any semantic changes to canonical-constraint handling.
ideas.why_this_is_simpler: It is a documentation-only clarification that reduces future complexity pressure instead of introducing new abstraction work.

expected_benefits: Keeps future simplification efforts from accidentally collapsing a thesis-sensitive distinction.
Reduces re-litigation of already-resolved prelude duplication work.
Provides a clear local reminder near affected code or notes.
ideas.problem: The evidence says the raw-vs-finalized canonical-constraint split is thesis-sensitive and must be preserved, and round 1 already removed shared-prelude duplication without changing that split. A bounded simplification candidate is to codify this as an explicit non-goal near remaining simplification hotspots, so later cleanup does not drift into semantic unification.
required_validation: Confirm the wording matches the established thesis-sensitive distinction exactly.
Place the note only where it guards active simplification hotspots rather than scattering redundant comments.
Verify it does not contradict the already-completed round-1 prelude cleanup.
risks: This may add comment/docs churn without direct code reduction.
Poor wording could be misread as freezing adjacent safe cleanups that are still allowed.
ideas.title: Document the canonical raw-vs-finalized split as an explicit non-goal for further cleanup
ideas.why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: Preserving thesis exactness is the whole point of this candidate: it records that simplification must not collapse the raw and finalized canonical-constraint phases.
ideas.why_this_is_simpler: Instead of changing semantics, it bounds future work by stating what must stay separate, making other small cleanups safer to discuss.
